verses.text: The LORD said: hew thee two tables of stone like unto the first, come up to Me; make thee an ark of wood. I will write the words that were in the first tables which thou brakest. I hewed two tables, went up. He wrote the ten commandments. I came down, put the tables in the ark; there they be. Guidance: A second chance written on stone twice. The first set Moses broke; the second set he carries into a wooden ark. God writes the same words AGAIN — He doesn't downgrade the covenant after the calf. Grace doesn't lower the standard; it reissues it.

Israel journeyed to Mosera: there Aaron died, was buried; Eleazar his son ministered in his stead. They journeyed to Gudgodah, then Jotbath, a land of rivers of waters. At that time the LORD separated Levi to bear the ark, to stand before the LORD, to bless in His name. Levi hath no part nor inheritance with his brethren; the LORD is his inheritance. Guidance: Aaron passes off-stage in a single line, and the priesthood continues without missing a service. Levi gets no land — God Himself is their portion. The most landless tribe is the richest tribe in the only currency that lasts.

I stayed in the mount, according to the first time, forty days and forty nights; the LORD hearkened unto me at that time also, the LORD would not destroy thee. The LORD said: arise, take thy journey before the people, that they may go in and possess the land. Guidance: Forty days twice over — Moses' total intercession was eighty days without food. And God's verdict: I will not destroy thee. The journey continues, the people go in. Behind every march forward is somebody's hidden fast.

And now, Israel, what doth the LORD thy God require of thee — but to fear the LORD thy God, to walk in all His ways, and to love Him, and to serve the LORD thy God with all thy heart and with all thy soul, to keep the commandments and statutes which I command thee this day for thy good? Guidance: Five verbs hold the entire law together — fear, walk, love, serve, keep. And the punchline: it's all 'for thy good.' Obedience is not God's appetite; it is His prescription. He asks what He asks because it heals.

Behold, the heaven and the heaven of heavens is the LORD's thy God, the earth also with all that therein is. Only the LORD had a delight in thy fathers to love them, He chose their seed after them, even you above all people. Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart, and be no more stiffnecked. Guidance: He owns the cosmos and chose your grandfather. The proper response to that affection is not just outward circumcision but inward — let Him cut the callus off your heart. The opposite of stiffneck is soft heart, and only God can make it.

For the LORD your God is God of gods, Lord of lords, a great God, mighty, and terrible, which regardeth not persons, nor taketh reward. He doth execute the judgment of the fatherless and widow, and loveth the stranger, in giving him food and raiment. Love ye therefore the stranger: for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt. Guidance: The God who outranks everything is the same God who hand-delivers bread to refugees. No bribe will buy Him; no orphan is invisible to Him. And the directive lands: love the stranger because you were one. Memory of exile must produce mercy, not amnesia.

Thou shalt fear the LORD; Him shalt thou serve; to Him shalt thou cleave, and swear by His name. He is thy praise, and He is thy God, that hath done for thee these great and terrible things. Thy fathers went down into Egypt with threescore and ten persons; and now the LORD hath made thee as the stars of heaven for multitude. Guidance: From seventy to stars. The man who started with one family is now a nation. Cleave to the One who multiplied you; serve the One who did the math. Praise is not flattery — it is the right-naming of what you have seen.
